System.Identity-1.0.0/Scopes-1.0.0.description: Models the scopes this API resource allows.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowedAccessToken.description: Signing algorithm for access token. If empty, will use the server default signing algorithm.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ShowInDiscoveryDoc.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client will be shown in the discovery document. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IsEmphasized-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether the consent screen will emphasize this scope (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IsRequired-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether the user can de-select the scope on the consent screen (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ShowInDiscoveryDoc.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client will be shown in the discovery document. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Authority-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the URI of the provider, or discovery endpoint for OpenID Connect discovery.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientSecret-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the client secret of the application.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientUri-1.0.0.description: URI to further information about client (used on consent screen)

System.Identity-1.0.0/LogoUri-1.0.0.description: URI to client logo (used on consent screen)

System.Identity-1.0.0/RequireConsent-1.0.description: Specifies whether a consent screen is required. Defaults to false.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowRememberConse.description: Specifies whether user can choose to store consent decisions (defaults to true)

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowedGrantTypes-.description: Specifies the allowed grant types (legal combinations of AuthorizationCode, Implicit, Hybrid, ResourceOwner, ClientCredentials). Defaults to Implicit.

System.Identity-1.0.0/RequirePkce-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether a proof key is required for authorization code based token requests (defaults to true).

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowPlainTextPkce.description: Specifies whether a proof key can be sent using plain method (not recommended and defaults to false).

System.Identity-1.0.0/RequireRequestObje.description: Specifies whether the client must use a request object on authorize requests (defaults to false).

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowAccessTokensV.description: Controls whether access tokens are transmitted via the browser for this client

System.Identity-1.0.0/RequireDPoP-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether a DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession) token is required to be used by this client (defaults to false)

System.Identity-1.0.0/DPoPValidationMode.description: Enum setting to control validation for the DPoP proof token expiration.

System.Identity-1.0.0/DPoPClockSkew-1.0..description: Clock skew used in validating the client's DPoP proof token 'iat' claim value. Defaults to 5 minutes.

System.Identity-1.0.0/RedirectUris-1.0.0.description: Specifies allowed URIs to return tokens or authorization codes to.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PostLogoutRedirect.description: Specifies allowed URIs to redirect to after logout

System.Identity-1.0.0/FrontChannelLogout.description: Specifies logout URI at client for HTTP front-channel based logout.
Specifies if the user's session id should be sent to the FrontChannelLogoutUri. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/BackChannelLogoutU.description: Specifies logout URI at client for HTTP back-channel based logout.

System.Identity-1.0.0/BackChannelLogoutS.description: Specifies if the user's session id should be sent to the BackChannelLogoutUri. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowOfflineAccess.description: Specifies whether this client can request refresh tokens (defaults to false)

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowedScopes-1.0..description: Specifies the api scopes that the client is allowed to request. If empty, the client can't access any scope

System.Identity-1.0.0/AlwaysIncludeUserC.description: When requesting both an id token and access token, should the user claims always be added to the id token instead of requiring the client to use the userinfo endpoint.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IdentityTokenLifet.description: Lifetime of identity token in seconds (defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes)

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowedIdentityTok.description: Signing algorithm for identity token. If empty, will use the server default signing algorithm.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AccessTokenLifetim.description: Lifetime of access token in seconds (defaults to 3600 seconds / 1 hour)

System.Identity-1.0.0/AuthorizationCodeL.description: Lifetime of authorization code in seconds (defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes)

System.Identity-1.0.0/AbsoluteRefreshTok.description: Maximum lifetime of a refresh token in seconds. Defaults to 2592000 seconds / 30 days

System.Identity-1.0.0/SlidingRefreshToke.description: Sliding lifetime of a refresh token in seconds. Defaults to 1296000 seconds / 15 days

System.Identity-1.0.0/ConsentLifetime-1..description: Lifetime of a user consent in seconds. Defaults to null (no expiration)

System.Identity-1.0.0/UpdateAccessTokenC.description: Indicates whether the access token (and its claims) should be updated on a refresh token request.

System.Identity-1.0.0/RefreshTokenExpira.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the access token (and its claims) should be updated on a refresh token request.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AccessTokenType-1..description: Specifies whether the access token is a reference token or a self contained JWT token (defaults to Jwt).

System.Identity-1.0.0/EnableLocalLogin-1.description: Specifies if this client can use local accounts. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IdentityProviderRe.description: Specifies which external IdPs can be used with this client (if list is empty all IdPs are allowed). Defaults to empty.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IncludeJwtId-1.0.0.description: Value indicating whether JWT access tokens should include an identifier. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientClaims-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the client claims to be sent to the user info endpoint.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AlwaysSendClientCl.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether client claims should be always included in the access tokens - or only for client credentials flow.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientClaimsPrefix.description: Gets or sets a value to prefix it on client claim types. Defaults to client_.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PairWiseSubjectSal.description: Gets or sets a salt value used in pair-wise subjectId generation for users of this client.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UserSsoLifetime-1..description: Lifetime of user's single sign-on session (in seconds). Defaults to null (no expiration).

System.Identity-1.0.0/UserCodeType-1.0.0.description: Specifies the user code type for the device flow. Defaults to null.

System.Identity-1.0.0/DeviceCodeLifetime.description: Specifies the lifetime (in seconds) of the device code. Defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes.

System.Identity-1.0.0/CibaLifetime-1.0.0.description: The backchannel authentication request lifetime in seconds.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PollingInterval-1..description: The backchannel authentication request polling interval in seconds.

System.Identity-1.0.0/CoordinateLifetime.description: When enabled, the client's token lifetimes (e.g. refresh tokens) will be tied to the user's session lifetime.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AllowedCorsOrigins.description: Gets or sets the allowed CORS origins for JavaScript clients.

System.Identity-1.0.0/InitiateLoginUri-1.description: Gets of sets a URI that can be used to initiate login from the IdentityServer host or a third party.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientSecret-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the client secret of the application.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IsEmphasized-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether the consent screen will emphasize this scope (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.

System.Identity-1.0.0/IsRequired-1.0.0.description: Specifies whether the user can de-select the scope on the consent screen (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ShowInDiscoveryDoc.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client will be shown in the discovery document. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Host-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the host address of the identity provider.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UseTls-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets if the connection to the identity provider should use TLS. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Port-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the host port of the identity provider.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ClientSecret-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the client secret of the application.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Host-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the host address of the identity provider.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UseTls-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets if the connection to the identity provider should use TLS. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Port-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the host port of the identity provider.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UserBaseDn-1.0.0.description: Gets or sets the base DN of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UserNameAttribute-.description: Gets or sets the name of the user attribute.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ShowInDiscoveryDoc.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client will be shown in the discovery document. Defaults to true.

System.Identity-1.0.0/FirstName-1.0.0.description: The first name of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/LastName-1.0.0.description: The last name of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/Email-1.0.0.description: The email address of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/NormalizedEmail-1..description: The normalized email address of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/EmailConfirmed-1.0.description: Indicates if the email address of the user has been confirmed.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PasswordHash-1.0.0.description: The salted and hashed representation of the password for this user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/SecurityStamp-1.0..description: A random value that should change whenever a users credentials have been compromised.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PhoneNumber-1.0.0.description: The phone number of the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/PhoneNumberConfirm.description: Indicates if the phone number of the user has been confirmed.

System.Identity-1.0.0/TwoFactorEnabled-1.description: Indicates if two factor authentication is enabled for the user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/LockoutEnd-1.0.0.description: The date and time, in UTC, when any user lockout ends.

System.Identity-1.0.0/LockoutEnabled-1.0.description: Flag indicating if the user could be locked out.

System.Identity-1.0.0/AccessFailedCount-.description: The number of failed login attempts for the current user.

System.Identity-1.0.0/ResetPasswordOnLog.description: Force the user to change the password after the next login.

System.Identity-1.0.0/RoleIds-1.0.0.description: The id of roles the user is a member of.

System.Identity-1.0.0/UserLogins-1.0.0.description: The existing logins of the user.
